```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))

## Parametros
mu0 <- sample(c(3, 5, 8, 10), 1)

## Choices textuais
all_choices <- c(
  "$Z > z_{0{,}95} = 1{,}645$.",
  "$Z > z_{0{,}975} = 1{,}96$.",
  "$|Z| > z_{0{,}975} = 1{,}96$.",
  "$Z < -z_{0{,}95} = -1{,}645$.",
  "$Z > z_{0{,}99} = 2{,}326$."
)

perm    <- sample(length(all_choices))
choices <- all_choices[perm]
sol_pos <- which(perm == 1)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(perm) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Para o teste $H_0:\mu = `r mu0`$ vs. $H_1:\mu > `r mu0`$ (unilateral a direita) com nivel $\alpha = 0{,}05$, usando a estatistica $Z = \sqrt{n}(\bar{X}-\mu_0)/\sigma$, a regiao critica e:

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
Para $H_1:\mu > \mu_0$, a regiao critica e unilateral a direita: rejeita-se $H_0$ quando $Z > z_{1-\alpha} = z_{0{,}95} = 1{,}645$.

Usar $z_{0{,}975}=1{,}96$ seria para $\alpha=0{,}025$ unilateral ou $\alpha=0{,}05$ bilateral. A regiao $|Z|>1{,}96$ e bilateral. $Z<-1{,}645$ seria para $H_1:\mu < \mu_0$. $z_{0{,}99}=2{,}326$ corresponderia a $\alpha=0{,}01$.

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap6S1Q8_teste_unilateral_bilateral
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
